summary: Strong 1970 show with standout Cryptical→Other One, Black Peter and Baby Blue; mixed matrix recording – audience‑sourced intro and vocal‑heavy mid‑section but clear later tracks.
review: Fans rave about the rare Big Boy Pete, the intense Cryptical→Other One sequence, and a superb second set featuring Black Peter, Uncle John’s Band and Baby Blue. The recording is a hybrid matrix: the first two songs are audience‑mic only and the middle section suffers from a vocal‑forward mix that needs EQ, but the sound improves dramatically after that.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating is 4.25 ★; reviewers praise the musical performance (many 5‑star comments) while noting consistent recording flaws in the opening and mid‑section, yielding a weighted score around 4.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: First two tracks are audience recordings with poor mic quality; middle portion has an overly high vocal level and benefits from EQ; later tracks are clear and well‑balanced, though occasional drop‑outs are mentioned.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note a tight flow with a rare Big Boy Pete early on and a second‑set surge of energy; transitions into Black Peter, Uncle John’s Band and Baby Blue are highlighted as especially smooth.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone praised throughout; one reviewer wished it was a bit louder in the mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen's harmonica/harp work repeatedly called excellent and a highlight of the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Good Lovin' drums singled out as strong; overall drumming described as solid.
